meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for zk-rollup — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: Batch Hashing and Commitment Bugs
cards.detect: Batch Hashing and Commitment Bugs
check: VERIFY: Batch Hashing and Commitment Bugs
cards.validWhen: Batch Hashing and Commitment Bugs

cards.title: Pattern 1: Batcher Frame Decoding Inconsistency Causes Consensus Split
cards.detect: Optimism's batch decoding in `op-node` and node software must implement the exact same frame-decoding logic.
check: VERIFY: Optimism's batch decoding in op-node and node software must implement the exact same frame-decoding logic.
PROVE: Op-node batch decoder behavior differs from reference implementation
FALSIFY: Strict adherence to spec; comprehensive test suite including edge cases
cards.antipattern: Op-node batch decoder behavior differs from reference implementation
cards.securePattern: Strict adherence to spec; comprehensive test suite including edge cases
cards.validWhen: Optimism's batch decoding in op-node and node software must implement the exact same frame-decoding logic.
cards.invalidWhen: Strict adherence to spec; comprehensive test suite including edge cases

cards.title: Pattern 2: EIP-4844 Blob Incompatibility Halts Block Processing
cards.detect: EIP-4844 blobs use `blob_versioned_hash` (prefix `0x01`) to address blob data.
check: VERIFY: EIP-4844 blobs use blob_versioned_hash (prefix 0x01) to address blob data.
PROVE: L1 contract expects raw keccak hash, not blob versioned hash
FALSIFY: Validate blob versioned hash prefix and use BLOBHASH opcode
cards.antipattern: L1 contract expects raw keccak hash, not blob versioned hash
cards.securePattern: Validate blob versioned hash prefix and use BLOBHASH opcode
cards.validWhen: EIP-4844 blobs use blob_versioned_hash (prefix 0x01) to address blob data.
cards.invalidWhen: Validate blob versioned hash prefix and use BLOBHASH opcode

cards.title: Pattern 3: Rollup Cannot Split Batches Across Blobs → Block Stuffing
cards.detect: EIP-4844 blobs have a fixed maximum size (128KB).
check: VERIFY: EIP-4844 blobs have a fixed maximum size (128KB).
PROVE: rollup.sol only supports single-blob batches
FALSIFY: Support multi-blob batches with array of blob hashes
cards.antipattern: rollup.sol only supports single-blob batches
cards.securePattern: Support multi-blob batches with array of blob hashes
cards.validWhen: EIP-4844 blobs have a fixed maximum size (128KB).
cards.invalidWhen: Support multi-blob batches with array of blob hashes

cards.title: Pattern 4: Malformed Blob Transaction Crashes Validator Nodes
cards.detect: Some L2 validator clients (e.
check: VERIFY: Some L2 validator clients (e.g., Nethermind) have latent bugs in their EIP-4844 blob transaction parsing that surface only on specific malformed...
cards.validWhen: Some L2 validator clients (e.g., Nethermind) have latent bugs in their EIP-4844 blob transaction parsing that surface only on specific malformed input.

cards.title: Pattern 5: Memory Corruption Causing Incorrect Batch Hashes (Scroll)
cards.detect: In Scroll's batch hashing implementation, batch header data was assembled in memory with an off-by-one error in the...
check: VERIFY: In Scroll's batch hashing implementation, batch header data was assembled in memory with an off-by-one error in the field packing, causing some bytes...
PROVE: Incorrect memory encoding of batch header (Scroll)
FALSIFY: Use abi.encode for structured hashing to prevent manual offset errors
cards.antipattern: Incorrect memory encoding of batch header (Scroll)
cards.securePattern: Use abi.encode for structured hashing to prevent manual offset errors
cards.validWhen: In Scroll's batch hashing implementation, batch header data was assembled in memory with an off-by-one error in the field packing, causing some bytes from one field to...
cards.invalidWhen: Use abi.encode for structured hashing to prevent manual offset errors

cards.title: Pattern 6: Incorrect basefee Calculation on Taiko Rollup
cards.detect: Taiko's L2 basefee follows EIP-1559 but adjusts based on two inputs: actual L2 gas used and configured gas target.
check: VERIFY: Taiko's L2 basefee follows EIP-1559 but adjusts based on two inputs: actual L2 gas used and configured gas target.
PROVE: Uses anchorGasTarget instead of the configured target
cards.antipattern: Uses anchorGasTarget instead of the configured target
cards.validWhen: Taiko's L2 basefee follows EIP-1559 but adjusts based on two inputs: actual L2 gas used and configured gas target.

cards.title: Pattern 7: inChallenge Incorrectly Reset in revertBatch
cards.detect: The `inChallenge` flag is used to gate new batch commitments and prevent race conditions during challenge periods.
check: VERIFY: inChallenge flag is used to gate new batch commitments and prevent race conditions during challenge periods.
PROVE: inChallenge reset too early in revertBatch
FALSIFY: Reset inChallenge only after full cleanup
cards.antipattern: inChallenge reset too early in revertBatch
cards.securePattern: Reset inChallenge only after full cleanup
cards.validWhen: The inChallenge flag is used to gate new batch commitments and prevent race conditions during challenge periods.
cards.invalidWhen: Reset inChallenge only after full cleanup

cards.title: Token Accounting and Token Mapping Errors
cards.detect: Token Accounting and Token Mapping Errors
check: VERIFY: Token Accounting and Token Mapping Errors
cards.validWhen: Token Accounting and Token Mapping Errors

cards.title: Message Channel DoS and Censorship
cards.detect: Message Channel DoS and Censorship
check: VERIFY: Message Channel DoS and Censorship
cards.validWhen: Message Channel DoS and Censorship

cards.title: Pattern 1: Token Bridge Reentrancy Corrupts Accounting
cards.detect: Bridge processes deposit with external call before state update
check: VERIFY: Bridge processes deposit with external call before state update
PROVE: User calls bridge with an ERC777 or callback-capable token (e.g., ERC1363); Bridge receives tokens and starts processing the deposit
FALSIFY: Update state BEFORE making any external calls (CEI pattern)
cards.antipattern: Bridge processes deposit with external call before state update
cards.securePattern: Update state BEFORE making any external calls (CEI pattern)
cards.validWhen: Bridge processes deposit with external call before state update
cards.invalidWhen: Update state BEFORE making any external calls (CEI pattern)

cards.title: Pattern 2: Wrong Token Order in Bridge Causes Incorrect Routing
cards.detect: Bridges that handle token pairs (e.
check: VERIFY: Bridges that handle token pairs (e.g., yield-bearing USD tokens: USDB/USDW) sometimes accept token A and B as arguments but switch their positions...
PROVE: L1BlastBridge swaps token order when emitting event
FALSIFY: Verify token arguments are in correct order
cards.antipattern: L1BlastBridge swaps token order when emitting event
cards.securePattern: Verify token arguments are in correct order
cards.validWhen: Bridges that handle token pairs (e.g., yield-bearing USD tokens: USDB/USDW) sometimes accept token A and B as arguments but switch their positions internally.
cards.invalidWhen: Verify token arguments are in correct order

cards.title: Pattern 3: Router Signatures Replay Across Chains
cards.detect: Cross-chain routers use off-chain signatures to authorize message execution.
check: VERIFY: Cross-chain routers use off-chain signatures to authorize message execution.
PROVE: Signed message has no domain separator
FALSIFY: Include EIP-712 domain separator in signed digest
cards.antipattern: Signed message has no domain separator
cards.securePattern: Include EIP-712 domain separator in signed digest
cards.validWhen: Cross-chain routers use off-chain signatures to authorize message execution.
cards.invalidWhen: Include EIP-712 domain separator in signed digest

cards.title: Pattern 4: Wrong ERC1155 Selector Locks Tokens in Bridge
cards.detect: ERC1155 has two transfer functions: `safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256,uint256,bytes)` and `safeBatchTransferFrom(...)`.
check: VERIFY: ERC1155 has two transfer functions: safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256,uint256,bytes) and safeBatchTransferFrom(...).
PROVE: Bridge uses wrong selector for ERC1155 safeTransferFrom
FALSIFY: Use correct ERC1155 function signature
cards.antipattern: Bridge uses wrong selector for ERC1155 safeTransferFrom
cards.securePattern: Use correct ERC1155 function signature
cards.validWhen: ERC1155 has two transfer functions: safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256,uint256,bytes) and safeBatchTransferFrom(...).
cards.invalidWhen: Use correct ERC1155 function signature

cards.title: Pattern 5: USDC Blacklist Permanently Locks Bridge Funds
cards.detect: USDC has a blacklist mechanism: blacklisted addresses cannot receive USDC transfers (the `transfer` call reverts).
check: VERIFY: USDC has a blacklist mechanism: blacklisted addresses cannot receive USDC transfers (the transfer call reverts).
PROVE: Bridge adapter doesn't handle USDC blacklist
FALSIFY: Implement fallback for USDC transfer failures
cards.antipattern: Bridge adapter doesn't handle USDC blacklist
cards.securePattern: Implement fallback for USDC transfer failures
cards.validWhen: USDC has a blacklist mechanism: blacklisted addresses cannot receive USDC transfers (the transfer call reverts).
cards.invalidWhen: Implement fallback for USDC transfer failures

cards.title: Pattern 6: Single Reverting Withdrawal Blocks Entire Queue
cards.detect: Vault or bridge withdrawal queues are processed in order (FIFO).
check: VERIFY: Vault or bridge withdrawal queues are processed in order (FIFO).
PROVE: Process all withdrawals in a loop — any revert blocks queue
FALSIFY: Skip failing withdrawals (try/catch) and record for manual resolution
cards.antipattern: Process all withdrawals in a loop — any revert blocks queue
cards.securePattern: Skip failing withdrawals (try/catch) and record for manual resolution
cards.validWhen: Vault or bridge withdrawal queues are processed in order (FIFO).
cards.invalidWhen: Skip failing withdrawals (try/catch) and record for manual resolution

cards.title: Missing PIL / AIR Constraints
cards.detect: Missing PIL / AIR Constraints
check: VERIFY: Missing PIL / AIR Constraints
cards.validWhen: Missing PIL / AIR Constraints

cards.title: Pattern 1: Missing PIL Constraint for SMT Inclusion
cards.detect: storage.pil - Missing constraint on SMT sibling node relationship
check: VERIFY: storage.pil - Missing constraint on SMT sibling node relationship
PROVE: Attacker targets a PIL constraint file (e.g., storage.pil) that verifies Sparse Merkle Tree inclusion proofs; Due to a missing polynomial identity, the prover can assign...
FALSIFY: Add constraint ensuring sibling relationship is enforced at each level
cards.antipattern: storage.pil - Missing constraint on SMT sibling node relationship
cards.securePattern: Add constraint ensuring sibling relationship is enforced at each level
cards.validWhen: storage.pil - Missing constraint on SMT sibling node relationship
cards.invalidWhen: Add constraint ensuring sibling relationship is enforced at each level

cards.title: Pattern 2: Underconstrained Carry Value in Binary State Machine
cards.detect: binary.pil - carry bit not range-constrained
check: VERIFY: binary.pil - carry bit not range-constrained
PROVE: The binary state machine processes bitwise operations (AND, OR, XOR); The carry bit between operations is allocated as a witness variable but never constrained to {0, 1}
FALSIFY: Add Boolean constraint on carry
cards.antipattern: binary.pil - carry bit not range-constrained
cards.securePattern: Add Boolean constraint on carry
cards.validWhen: binary.pil - carry bit not range-constrained
cards.invalidWhen: Add Boolean constraint on carry

cards.title: Pattern 3: Missing Range Constraint on Division Remainder (zkEVM Opcode)
cards.detect: When implementing integer division (`a / b = q, remainder r`) in a ZK circuit, the standard approach allocates witness...
check: VERIFY: When implementing integer division (a / b = q, remainder r) in a ZK circuit, the standard approach allocates witness variables for q and r, then...
PROVE: Adversary crafts a transaction that performs a specific division (e.g., SHR r1, 1); The witness generation produces incorrect quotient = 0 for a specially crafted value...
FALSIFY: Add range constraint: remainder < divisor
ANTIPATTERN: zkevm_circuits/src/main_vm/opcodes/mul_div.rs
cards.antipattern: zkevm_circuits/src/main_vm/opcodes/mul_div.rs
cards.securePattern: Add range constraint: remainder < divisor
cards.validWhen: When implementing integer division (a / b = q, remainder r) in a ZK circuit, the standard approach allocates witness variables for q and r, then constrains: q * b + r =...
cards.invalidWhen: Add range constraint: remainder < divisor

cards.title: Pattern 4: IsLtArraySubAir Soundness Issue (RISC-V Circuit)
cards.detect: The `IsLtArraySubAir` circuit is supposed to compare two arrays lexicographically and output `true` if the first is...
check: VERIFY: IsLtArraySubAir circuit is supposed to compare two arrays lexicographically and output true if the first is strictly less than the second.
FALSIFY: Enforce that diff_marker[i] = 1 implies a[i] != b[i]
ANTIPATTERN: IsLtArraySubAir
cards.antipattern: IsLtArraySubAir
cards.securePattern: Enforce that diff_marker[i] = 1 implies a[i] != b[i]
cards.validWhen: The IsLtArraySubAir circuit is supposed to compare two arrays lexicographically and output true if the first is strictly less than the second.
cards.invalidWhen: Enforce that diff_marker[i] = 1 implies a[i] != b[i]

cards.title: Pattern 5: SHA256 AIR Unconstrained final_hash at Last Block
cards.detect: The `sha256-air` processes input data in 64-byte blocks.
check: VERIFY: sha256-air processes input data in 64-byte blocks.
FALSIFY: Add constraint at last block row
cards.securePattern: Add constraint at last block row
cards.validWhen: The sha256-air processes input data in 64-byte blocks.
cards.invalidWhen: Add constraint at last block row

cards.title: Pattern 6: partial_sha256_var_interstitial Hash Collision (Undersized Input)
cards.detect: `partial_sha256_var_interstitial` hashes `BLOCK_SIZE * floor(N / BLOCK_SIZE)` bytes, not `N` bytes.
check: VERIFY: partial_sha256_var_interstitial hashes BLOCK_SIZE * floor(N / BLOCK_SIZE) bytes, not N bytes.
PROVE: partial_sha256_var_interstitial
cards.antipattern: partial_sha256_var_interstitial
cards.validWhen: partial_sha256_var_interstitial hashes BLOCK_SIZE * floor(N / BLOCK_SIZE) bytes, not N bytes.

cards.title: Pattern 7: JALR imm_sign Unconstrained (RISC-V zkVM)
cards.detect: In the JALR opcode circuit, there is an `imm_sign` flag that controls whether the 12-bit immediate is sign-extended.
check: VERIFY: In the JALR opcode circuit, there is an imm_sign flag that controls whether the 12-bit immediate is sign-extended.
FALSIFY: Constrain imm_sign to equal the MSB of the immediate
ANTIPATTERN: jalr/core.rs
cards.antipattern: jalr/core.rs
cards.securePattern: Constrain imm_sign to equal the MSB of the immediate
cards.validWhen: In the JALR opcode circuit, there is an imm_sign flag that controls whether the 12-bit immediate is sign-extended.
cards.invalidWhen: Constrain imm_sign to equal the MSB of the immediate

cards.title: CREATE / CREATE2 / CREATE3 Incompatibilities
cards.detect: CREATE / CREATE2 / CREATE3 Incompatibilities
check: VERIFY: CREATE / CREATE2 / CREATE3 Incompatibilities
cards.validWhen: CREATE / CREATE2 / CREATE3 Incompatibilities

cards.title: Opcode and Precompile Divergences
cards.detect: Opcode and Precompile Divergences
check: VERIFY: Opcode and Precompile Divergences
cards.validWhen: Opcode and Precompile Divergences

cards.title: msg.sender and Context Differences
cards.detect: msg.sender and Context Differences
check: VERIFY: msg.sender and Context Differences
cards.validWhen: msg.sender and Context Differences

cards.title: Bytecode Compression
cards.detect: Bytecode Compression
cards.validWhen: Bytecode Compression

cards.title: Pattern 1: CREATE2 Address Derivation Differs on ZKSync
cards.detect: Uniswap V3-style pool address computation
check: VERIFY: Uniswap V3-style pool address computation
PROVE: Protocol uses CREATE2 with a factory contract to deploy predictable-address proxies or pool contracts; Frontend / off-chain tooling computes the expected deployment...
FALSIFY: Use ZKSync-specific address derivation when deploying on ZKSync
cards.antipattern: Uniswap V3-style pool address computation
cards.securePattern: Use ZKSync-specific address derivation when deploying on ZKSync
cards.validWhen: Uniswap V3-style pool address computation
cards.invalidWhen: Use ZKSync-specific address derivation when deploying on ZKSync

cards.title: Pattern 2: ecrecover Discrepancy in delegatecall Context on ZKSync
cards.detect: On Ethereum, when contract A `delegatecall`s contract B, and B calls the `ecrecover` precompile, the call context...
check: VERIFY: On Ethereum, when contract A delegatecalls contract B, and B calls the ecrecover precompile, the call context (including caller identity) is...
PROVE: Library using ecrecover — correct on Ethereum, wrong on ZKSync
FALSIFY: On ZKSync, call ecrecover directly (not via delegatecall)
cards.antipattern: Library using ecrecover — correct on Ethereum, wrong on ZKSync
cards.securePattern: On ZKSync, call ecrecover directly (not via delegatecall)
cards.validWhen: On Ethereum, when contract A delegatecalls contract B, and B calls the ecrecover precompile, the call context (including caller identity) is correctly preserved.
cards.invalidWhen: On ZKSync, call ecrecover directly (not via delegatecall)

cards.title: Pattern 3: Unauthorized Precompile Authorization Bypass via delegatecall
cards.detect: ZKSync system contracts have an authorization mechanism allowing only certain callers to invoke restricted precompile...
check: VERIFY: ZKSync system contracts have an authorization mechanism allowing only certain callers to invoke restricted precompile functions.
PROVE: ZKSync precompile checks msg.sender but not tx.origin
cards.antipattern: ZKSync precompile checks msg.sender but not tx.origin
cards.validWhen: ZKSync system contracts have an authorization mechanism allowing only certain callers to invoke restricted precompile functions.

cards.title: Pattern 4: Nonce Doesn't Increment for Reverted Child Deployments
cards.detect: On Ethereum (post-EIP-161), a contract's deployment nonce is incremented **before** the inner `CREATE` executes, so a...
check: VERIFY: On Ethereum (post-EIP-161), a contract's deployment nonce is incremented before the inner CREATE executes, so a reverted deployment still increments...
cards.validWhen: On Ethereum (post-EIP-161), a contract's deployment nonce is incremented before the inner CREATE executes, so a reverted deployment still increments the parent's nonce.

cards.title: Pattern 5: block.number Returns L1 Block Number on Arbitrum (Not L2)
cards.detect: On Arbitrum, `block.number` returns the **L1 block number** (approximately every 15 seconds), not the L2 block number...
check: VERIFY: On Arbitrum, block.number returns the L1 block number (approximately every 15 seconds), not the L2 block number (which advances with every L2...
PROVE: Protocol uses block.number for interest accrual
FALSIFY: Use block.timestamp for time-based calculations on L2
cards.antipattern: Protocol uses block.number for interest accrual
cards.securePattern: Use block.timestamp for time-based calculations on L2
cards.validWhen: On Arbitrum, block.number returns the L1 block number (approximately every 15 seconds), not the L2 block number (which advances with every L2 transaction).
cards.invalidWhen: Use block.timestamp for time-based calculations on L2

cards.title: Dispute Game Manipulation
cards.detect: Dispute Game Manipulation
check: VERIFY: Dispute Game Manipulation
cards.validWhen: Dispute Game Manipulation

cards.title: State Root and Batch Challenge Issues
cards.detect: State Root and Batch Challenge Issues
check: VERIFY: State Root and Batch Challenge Issues
cards.validWhen: State Root and Batch Challenge Issues

cards.title: Pattern 1: Attacker Freezes Chain via Fake prevStateRoot
cards.detect: Rollup contract doesn't validate prevStateRoot exists in history
check: VERIFY: Rollup contract doesn't validate prevStateRoot exists in history
PROVE: Attacker monitors the rollup for opportunities to submit a new batch with a crafted prevStateRoot; The prevStateRoot references a state that does not actually exist in...
FALSIFY: Enforce prevStateRoot is from the canonical chain
cards.antipattern: Rollup contract doesn't validate prevStateRoot exists in history
cards.securePattern: Enforce prevStateRoot is from the canonical chain
cards.validWhen: Rollup contract doesn't validate prevStateRoot exists in history
cards.invalidWhen: Enforce prevStateRoot is from the canonical chain

cards.title: Pattern 2: Challenger Misses Discrepancy Events → Malicious Execution
cards.detect: The challenger (fraud proof monitor) relies on on-chain events to detect discrepancies between proposed state...
check: VERIFY: challenger (fraud proof monitor) relies on on-chain events to detect discrepancies between proposed state transitions and the actual L2 computation.
PROVE: Discrepancy event only emitted for one execution type
cards.antipattern: Discrepancy event only emitted for one execution type
cards.validWhen: The challenger (fraud proof monitor) relies on on-chain events to detect discrepancies between proposed state transitions and the actual L2 computation.

cards.title: Pattern 3: opFaultVerifier Ingests Games That Resolve Incorrectly
cards.detect: The `OPFaultVerifier` (or similar finalization contract) reads the outcome of a `FaultDisputeGame` to determine if a...
check: VERIFY: OPFaultVerifier (or similar finalization contract) reads the outcome of a FaultDisputeGame to determine if a proposed output root should be accepted.
PROVE: OPFaultVerifier checks game existence but not winning party
FALSIFY: Check game was resolved in favor of the correct party
cards.antipattern: OPFaultVerifier checks game existence but not winning party
cards.securePattern: Check game was resolved in favor of the correct party
cards.validWhen: The OPFaultVerifier (or similar finalization contract) reads the outcome of a FaultDisputeGame to determine if a proposed output root should be accepted.
cards.invalidWhen: Check game was resolved in favor of the correct party

cards.title: Pattern 4: MIPS VM Panic Causes Unchallengeable Output Root
cards.detect: The optimistic rollup's fault proof uses an on-chain MIPS VM to simulate a single disputed instruction.
check: VERIFY: optimistic rollup's fault proof uses an on-chain MIPS VM to simulate a single disputed instruction.
PROVE: MIPS VM can panic on certain opcode inputs
FALSIFY: Validate all array indices before access; return error state instead of panicking
cards.antipattern: MIPS VM can panic on certain opcode inputs
cards.securePattern: Validate all array indices before access; return error state instead of panicking
cards.validWhen: The optimistic rollup's fault proof uses an on-chain MIPS VM to simulate a single disputed instruction.
cards.invalidWhen: Validate all array indices before access; return error state instead of panicking

cards.title: Pattern 5: Bond Slashed for Correct/Honest Assertion
cards.detect: In assertion-based fraud proof systems (e.
check: VERIFY: In assertion-based fraud proof systems (e.g., Arbitrum BOLD), each participant puts up a bond that can be slashed if their assertion is proven wrong.
PROVE: Bond slashing uses stale game state during challenge rounds
cards.antipattern: Bond slashing uses stale game state during challenge rounds
cards.validWhen: In assertion-based fraud proof systems (e.g., Arbitrum BOLD), each participant puts up a bond that can be slashed if their assertion is proven wrong.

cards.title: Pattern 6: Batches Committed During Challenge Avoid Being Challenged
cards.detect: Some rollup implementations use an `inChallenge` flag to prevent commitment of new batches while a challenge is ongoing.
check: VERIFY: Some rollup implementations use an inChallenge flag to prevent commitment of new batches while a challenge is ongoing.
PROVE: inChallenge only blocks future challenges, not new commitments
cards.antipattern: inChallenge only blocks future challenges, not new commitments
cards.validWhen: Some rollup implementations use an inChallenge flag to prevent commitment of new batches while a challenge is ongoing.

cards.title: Gas Calculation Errors
cards.detect: Gas Calculation Errors
check: VERIFY: Gas Calculation Errors
cards.validWhen: Gas Calculation Errors

cards.detect: Fee Theft and Manipulation
check: VERIFY: Fee Theft and Manipulation
cards.validWhen: Fee Theft and Manipulation

cards.title: Pattern 1: Paymaster Refunds spentOnPubdata Instead of Burning
cards.detect: In ZKSync, `spentOnPubdata` represents the gas consumed by posting state diffs to L1 (pubdata).
check: VERIFY: In ZKSync, spentOnPubdata represents the gas consumed by posting state diffs to L1 (pubdata).
PROVE: Bootloader refunds full gas including pubdata cost
FALSIFY: Burn spentOnPubdata; only refund pure L2 gas not consumed
cards.antipattern: Bootloader refunds full gas including pubdata cost
cards.securePattern: Burn spentOnPubdata; only refund pure L2 gas not consumed
cards.validWhen: In ZKSync, spentOnPubdata represents the gas consumed by posting state diffs to L1 (pubdata).
cards.invalidWhen: Burn spentOnPubdata; only refund pure L2 gas not consumed

cards.title: Pattern 2: Operator Steals All Gas Provided for L1→L2 Transactions
cards.detect: When a user sends an L1→L2 transaction, they specify `gasLimit` and `gasPerPubdataByte`.
check: VERIFY: When a user sends an L1→L2 transaction, they specify gasLimit and gasPerPubdataByte.
PROVE: L1 contract trusts operator-provided gas parameters
FALSIFY: Enforce minimum gasPerPubdataByteLimit
cards.antipattern: L1 contract trusts operator-provided gas parameters
cards.securePattern: Enforce minimum gasPerPubdataByteLimit
cards.validWhen: When a user sends an L1→L2 transaction, they specify gasLimit and gasPerPubdataByte.
cards.invalidWhen: Enforce minimum gasPerPubdataByteLimit

cards.title: Pattern 3: Gas Calculation Uses Unchecked Free Variables
cards.detect: In STARK/ZK proof systems compiled from high-level languages, some intermediate variables in gas calculations may not...
check: VERIFY: In STARK/ZK proof systems compiled from high-level languages, some intermediate variables in gas calculations may not be constrained by the proof...
cards.validWhen: In STARK/ZK proof systems compiled from high-level languages, some intermediate variables in gas calculations may not be constrained by the proof (they are "free" or...

cards.title: Pattern 4: Burning User Gas in sendCompressedBytecode
cards.detect: When a new contract is deployed on ZKSync Era, the bytecode may need to be compressed and sent to L1 as part of pubdata.
check: VERIFY: When a new contract is deployed on ZKSync Era, the bytecode may need to be compressed and sent to L1 as part of pubdata.
PROVE: sendCompressedBytecode() called in user-funded transaction context
cards.antipattern: sendCompressedBytecode() called in user-funded transaction context
cards.validWhen: When a new contract is deployed on ZKSync Era, the bytecode may need to be compressed and sent to L1 as part of pubdata.

cards.title: Pattern 5: Incorrect commitScalar Underpays Sequencer
cards.detect: When computing how much the protocol should reimburse the sequencer for posting batch commitments to L1, a...
check: VERIFY: When computing how much the protocol should reimburse the sequencer for posting batch commitments to L1, a commitScalar constant is used...
PROVE: commitScalar is miscalculated — too low
FALSIFY: commitScalar includes all L1 calldata overhead
cards.antipattern: commitScalar is miscalculated — too low
cards.securePattern: commitScalar includes all L1 calldata overhead
cards.validWhen: When computing how much the protocol should reimburse the sequencer for posting batch commitments to L1, a commitScalar constant is used (representing the approximate L1...
cards.invalidWhen: commitScalar includes all L1 calldata overhead

cards.title: Pattern 6: Batch Fees Multiplier Cap Bypassed with Multiple Calls
cards.detect: A batch fee multiplier is used to limit fee escalation.
check: VERIFY: A batch fee multiplier is used to limit fee escalation.
PROVE: Cap enforced per-call, compounding across multiple calls
FALSIFY: Apply cap relative to a reference point that resets periodically (per block/epoch)
cards.antipattern: Cap enforced per-call, compounding across multiple calls
cards.securePattern: Apply cap relative to a reference point that resets periodically (per block/epoch)
cards.validWhen: A batch fee multiplier is used to limit fee escalation.
cards.invalidWhen: Apply cap relative to a reference point that resets periodically (per block/epoch)

cards.title: Pattern 7: Bytecode Compression Bypass Completeness Checks
cards.detect: Bytecode compression in ZKSync involves two steps: `convert` (encode dictionary) and `compress` (apply encoding).
check: VERIFY: Bytecode compression in ZKSync involves two steps: convert (encode dictionary) and compress (apply encoding).
PROVE: Compress called in same transaction as convert, skipping completeness
cards.antipattern: Compress called in same transaction as convert, skipping completeness
cards.validWhen: Bytecode compression in ZKSync involves two steps: convert (encode dictionary) and compress (apply encoding).

check: VERIFY: L1 → L2 Transaction Failures

cards.title: Pattern 1: Loss of Funds When L1→L2 Transaction Fails in Bootloader
cards.detect: L1 Mailbox doesn't guarantee refund on L2 execution failure
check: VERIFY: L1 Mailbox doesn't guarantee refund on L2 execution failure
PROVE: User sends ETH + L1→L2 message via requestL2Transaction() on L1; The transaction is successfully included in an L2 batch but fails during bootloader execution...
FALSIFY: Ensure refund recipient is explicitly set to an EOA or

cards.title: Pattern 3: Address Aliasing Locks ETH
cards.detect: When L1 contracts send ETH to L2 via `requestL2Transaction`, Ethereum applies "address aliasing": the L2 `msg.sender`...
check: VERIFY: When L1 contracts send ETH to L2 via requestL2Transaction, Ethereum applies "address aliasing": the L2 msg.sender is the L1 contract address +...

cards.title: Pattern 4: Attacker Fills L2ToL1MessagePasser Merkle Tree
cards.detect: The Optimism `L2ToL1MessagePasser` contract stores all L2→L1 withdrawal messages in a Merkle trie for later L1 proof verification.

cards.title: Pattern 2: Incorrect Randomness Computation Allows Proof Forgery

check: VERIFY: In interactive proof systems converted to non-interactive via Fiat-Shamir, the "random" challenge must be derived from all previous transcript values.
PROVE: Prover generates multiple commitments and includes them in the transcript; The Fiat-Shamir challenge is computed as H(C1, C2, ...) but due to a bug, some Ci are excluded
FALSIFY: Include ALL public commitments in the Fiat-Shamir transcript
ANTIPATTERN: Incorrect randomness — missing some commitments in transcript
cards.antipattern: Incorrect randomness — missing some commitments in transcript
cards.securePattern: Include ALL public commitments in the Fiat-Shamir transcript
cards.validWhen: In interactive proof systems converted to non-interactive via Fiat-Shamir, the "random" challenge must be derived from all previous transcript values.
cards.invalidWhen: Include ALL public commitments in the Fiat-Shamir transcript

check: VERIFY: In recursive proof systems (e.g., Groth16 with Fflonk recursion, Plonk with proof composition), the inner verifier circuit uses a "verification key...
PROVE: On-chain Groth16 verifier accepts prover-supplied VK root
FALSIFY: Hard-code the trusted VK root; reject proofs with different roots

cards.invalidWhen: Hard-code the trusted VK root; reject proofs with different roots

cards.title: Pattern 4: Weak Fiat-Shamir in LogUp Phase Enables Backdoored Circuits

cards.validWhen: LogUp (a lookup argument used in STARK/Plonky2 proofs) uses Fiat-Shamir to generate challenges for the LogUp sum-check.

cards.invalidWhen: Validate all public inputs are valid field elements

check: VERIFY: Sequencer Economic Issues

cards.title: Pattern 1: Access-Controlled Functions Fail During Sequencer Downtime
cards.detect: emergencyRepay() can only be called on L2
check: VERIFY: emergencyRepay() can only be called on L2
PROVE: Protocol has time-sensitive functions (e.g., debt repayment, collateral deposit, emergency pause); These functions require calling a smart contract on L2
FALSIFY: Add sequencer uptime check before enforcing deadlines
cards.antipattern: emergencyRepay() can only be called on L2
cards.securePattern: Add sequencer uptime check before enforcing deadlines
cards.validWhen: emergencyRepay() can only be called on L2
cards.invalidWhen: Add sequencer uptime check before enforcing deadlines

cards.title: Pattern 2: Dutch Auctions and Options Expire at Bad Prices During Downtime
cards.detect: Dutch auction price depends on block.
check: VERIFY: Dutch auction price depends on block.
PROVE: Protocol runs Dutch auctions (price decreases linearly over time) or time-limited options; L2 sequencer goes down during an active auction/option period
FALSIFY: Check sequencer is up (and has been up for grace period) before auctions
ANTIPATTERN: Dutch auction price depends on block.timestamp
cards.antipattern: Dutch auction price depends on block.timestamp
cards.securePattern: Check sequencer is up (and has been up for grace period) before auctions
cards.validWhen: Dutch auction price depends on block.
cards.invalidWhen: Check sequencer is up (and has been up for grace period) before auctions

cards.title: Pattern 3: Linea / Sequencer Censorship Locking User Funds
cards.detect: On some L2 chains (e.
check: VERIFY: On some L2 chains (e.g., early Linea), the sequencer has a denylist or role-based exclusion mechanism.
PROVE: Linea L2MessageService allows sequencer to denylist users
FALSIFY: Uncensorable withdrawal path — users can always force-exit to L1
cards.antipattern: Linea L2MessageService allows sequencer to denylist users
cards.securePattern: Uncensorable withdrawal path — users can always force-exit to L1
cards.validWhen: On some L2 chains (e.g., early Linea), the sequencer has a denylist or role-based exclusion mechanism.
cards.invalidWhen: Uncensorable withdrawal path — users can always force-exit to L1

cards.title: Pattern 4: Sequencer Underpaid Due to Incorrect commitScalar
cards.detect: ZK rollup sequencers (or keepers who submit proofs) are compensated for L1 gas costs via a `commitScalar` parameter...
check: VERIFY: ZK rollup sequencers (or keepers who submit proofs) are compensated for L1 gas costs via a commitScalar parameter encoded in transaction fee...
PROVE: commitScalar uses wrong L1 gas cost estimate
FALSIFY: Set commitScalar based on current L1 gas parameters and blob pricing
cards.antipattern: commitScalar uses wrong L1 gas cost estimate
cards.securePattern: Set commitScalar based on current L1 gas parameters and blob pricing
cards.validWhen: ZK rollup sequencers (or keepers who submit proofs) are compensated for L1 gas costs via a commitScalar parameter encoded in transaction fee calculations.
cards.invalidWhen: Set commitScalar based on current L1 gas parameters and blob pricing

cards.title: Pattern 5: Front-Running finalizeBlocks in Decentralized Sequencer Mode
cards.detect: In decentralized sequencer systems where multiple proposers can submit `finalizeBlocks`, the ordering of finalization...
check: VERIFY: In decentralized sequencer systems where multiple proposers can submit finalizeBlocks, the ordering of finalization transactions can be manipulated.
PROVE: Anyone can call finalizeBlocks for a batch
FALSIFY: Commit-reveal or permissioned submission prevents front-running
cards.antipattern: Anyone can call finalizeBlocks for a batch
cards.securePattern: Commit-reveal or permissioned submission prevents front-running
cards.validWhen: In decentralized sequencer systems where multiple proposers can submit finalizeBlocks, the ordering of finalization transactions can be manipulated.
cards.invalidWhen: Commit-reveal or permissioned submission prevents front-running
